population_threshold_relevance: Above both the 1.9M (fire mandatory arbitration, Sec.174.1535) and 1.5M (non-safety Ch.146) thresholds -- unique among Texas cities
police_fire_institutional_channel: Ch.174 full bargaining + compulsory arbitration (fire, HPFFA/IAFF 341); Ch.142 meet-and-confer (police, HPOU)
budget_or_pay_plan_targets: houstontx.gov/hr/compensation job-classification and pay-structure pages; Municipal Employee Guidebook
impasse_or_award_targets: Not yet located (Houston's compulsory-arbitration award history under Sec.174.1535 not searched this session)
rationale: Richest three-tier institutional structure in either state; anchor city for the first batch
risk_or_caveat: Two of three tiers are population-gated and not representative of sub-threshold cities; none of the located PDFs extracted as readable text this session (tooling limitation)
notes: See texas_ohio_legal_followup_source_audit_2026-07-08.md and texas_ohio_source_ingestion_audit_2026-07-08.csv for document-level detail already captured | APPROVED 2026-07-08: see texas_ohio_approved_source_plan_2026-07-08.csv for the authoritative, hygiene-clean first-batch row and metadata plan.

police_fire_institutional_channel: Ch.174 full bargaining (fire, Austin Firefighters Local 975); Ch.142 meet-and-confer (police, Austin Police Association)
non_safety_institutional_channel: Novel Feb-2026 AFSCME Local 1624 consultation agreement (non-statutory, first of its kind in Texas)
fire_source_targets: Austin Firefighters Local 975 CBA 2023-2025 + Oct-2025 proposed successor, austintexas.gov/department/labor-relations/fire-collective-bargaining-agreement
non_safety_source_targets: AFSCME Local 1624 consultation agreement (Feb 2026) -- specific document URL not yet located this session; afscme1624.org for union-side context
rationale: Demonstrates Houston's fire/police institutional split (Ch.174 fire + Ch.142 police) generalizes to a sub-threshold city one-third Houston's size, without needing any population-triggered exception
risk_or_caveat: Non-safety consultation agreement is very new (Feb 2026) and is not a Ch.146/142 statutory relationship -- may not satisfy this project's CBA source-verification standard as a matched non-safety document
notes: Best-organized single Texas city labor-relations portal found across all three TX/OH sessions to date | APPROVED 2026-07-08: see texas_ohio_approved_source_plan_2026-07-08.csv for the authoritative, hygiene-clean first-batch row and metadata plan.

police_fire_institutional_channel: Ch.174 full bargaining, both police (SAPOA) and fire (SAFD), non-compulsory
rationale: Clearest example of full Ch.174 bargaining operating without any population-triggered compulsory-arbitration exception -- a useful contrast to Houston Fire -- source_availability detail: high for police/fire (two CBAs on one centralized portal); none for non-safety; expected_design_value detail: high value specifically for the police/fire pair
risk_or_caveat: Non-safety comparison unit unidentified; 2021 Prop B ballot measure shows a contested political history for police bargaining status
notes: Central, dedicated sanantonio.gov/City-Attorney/CollectiveBargaining portal -- best-organized Texas portal for police+fire together

police_fire_institutional_channel: Ch.174 full bargaining (fire, Fort Worth Professional Firefighters Association); Ch.142 meet-and-confer (police, FWPOA, established via a 2006 referendum)
non_safety_institutional_channel: None confirmed (Pay for Performance Program for non-civil-service employees instead)
police_source_targets: FWPOA Meet and Confer Agreement -- current-cycle official copy not directly located this session; a 2017-2020 predecessor with salary schedule was found at apps.fortworthtexas.gov
fire_source_targets: Fire Collective Bargaining Contract -- referenced via fortworthtexas.gov/departments/citysecretary/events/collective-bargaining-fire-2024, full text not directly located this session
budget_or_pay_plan_targets: fortworthtexas.gov/departments/hr/administration/salary-schedule -- FY2024/2025/2026 citywide salary schedules officially hosted
rationale: Independently confirms the fire/police institutional split (full bargaining for fire, lighter meet-and-confer for police) found in Houston and Austin, in a third city -- source_availability detail: high for police/fire (dedicated portal, multi-year salary schedules); none for non-safety; expected_design_value detail: medium-high in the sense of confirming the split pattern, but non-safety channel is absent
risk_or_caveat: Current-cycle full CBA text for both police and fire not directly located this session, only referenced via city event pages; non-safety channel absent
notes: Fort Worth's City Secretary maintains dedicated yearly 'Collective Bargaining' event pages that likely link to full agreement text not directly followed this session

police_fire_institutional_channel: Ch.142 meet-and-confer, joint police+fire 'Meet and Confer Team' (not Ch.174)
non_safety_institutional_channel: None confirmed (civil-service classification system, ~1,100 classifications/8 salary schedules, instead; NTAPE Local 9487/CWA Local 6215 presence unconfirmed as a statutory channel)
police_source_targets: Joint police/fire Meet and Confer Agreement -- full text located via lris.com and a Ballotpedia-hosted PDF; an official dallascityhall.com copy not yet confirmed
fire_source_targets: (same joint agreement as police -- Dallas negotiates police and fire together, unlike Houston/Austin/Fort Worth's split)
rationale: Useful contrast for a joint (not split) police+fire meet-and-confer model, and a non-safety population with no confirmed statutory channel at all -- the cleanest example of the Ch.617 default persisting for non-safety employees -- source_availability detail: aggregator/Ballotpedia copies found; official city PDF unconfirmed
risk_or_caveat: No official-city-hosted full-text copy of the joint agreement confirmed yet; non-safety statutory channel absent
notes: NTAPE Local 9487 (USW) and CWA Local 6215 exist as employee organizations but neither was confirmed to have Ch.146-equivalent legal bargaining status

police_fire_institutional_channel: Ch.174 reportedly adopted (secondary-source only, a Texas Senate bill-analysis document listing El Paso among '30+' adopting cities)
police_source_targets: El Paso Municipal Police Officers' Association CBA 2023-2027 -- hosted by CLEAT (a police-union umbrella organization), not an official elpasotexas.gov copy
rationale: Corroborates that Ch.174 adoption is common across many Texas cities beyond the five core cities scanned, but not independently verified to this project's usual standard -- source_availability detail: secondary-sourced institutional status; union-hosted, not city-hosted, police CBA; expected_design_value detail: lowest independently-verified confidence of the six Texas cities scanned
risk_or_caveat: Institutional status rests on secondary sourcing only; the located police CBA is not an official city copy
notes: Added only for breadth; not recommended as a near-term acquisition target
